headline: 'We are walking a fine line': Incurably ill patients on France's new assisted-dying law
description: A law creating a right to aid in dying was adopted by French lawamkers on Wednesday. Le Monde spoke with people facing serious illness about how the choice weighs on their lives.

headline: Journalist Ali Lmrabet awaits possible trial in Morocco: 'I am accused of defaming the head of police and the king's chief adviser'
description: The French-Moroccan journalist was released from police custody on Wednesday following his arrest in Tangier on Sunday for 'the publication of defamatory and insulting digital content.'

headline: 'You're expected to perform, but no one wants to know how you do it': Cocaine, the toxic fuel of young professionals
description: Cocaine at work (1/3). Among young professionals, the illegal and highly addictive drug is used in a relentless pursuit of performance, often shrouded in taboo and without questioning of working conditions.

headline: Judge of 2016 Nice terror attack trial reflects on 'abominable' case, 10 years after
description: Laurent Raviot presided over the Paris special criminal court that held the trial of the attack in Nice that killed 86 people on July 14, 2016. On the 10th anniversary of the attack, he granted Le Monde his first interview, speaking with unusual candor.
